title: Seed Registry, Origin Scope, Budgets, and Stop Policy · Type-System Trace: Seed Registry
summary: A seed silently expands through subdomains, downloads, calendars, search forms, query permutations, and logout URLs without page, byte, time, or incident limits. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts seed registry increment in deny-by-default crawl-scope policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Bind each seed to owner authorization, purpose, expected content class, policy version, contact, and expiry.
Inventory streams, sockets, timers, browser contexts, files, listeners, workers, and close order with post-run handle evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
